title: Jwala Ji Kangra
tagline: Jwala Ji Kangra — spiritual town of Himachal Pradesh

overview.short: The Jwalamukhi (IAST: Jvālāmukhī) temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the goddess Jwala Devi, located in the Jawalamukhi town of Himachal Pradesh, India.
overview.description: The Jwalamukhi (IAST: Jvālāmukhī) temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the goddess Jwala Devi, located in the Jawalamukhi town of Himachal Pradesh, India. It is known for its eternal flame emanating from a rock fissure on top of which the main shrine has been built. It is considered one of the major Shakti pitha shrines.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Jawalamukhi
topPlaces.description: Jawalamukhi, also known as Jwalamukhi or Jawala-ji, is a temple town and a nagar parishad in Kangra district in the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. It is the location of the Jwalamukhi temple, which is known for a naturally burning flame emanating from a rock fissure, and recognized as one of the Shakta pithas.

topPlaces.name: Naleti
topPlaces.description: Naleti is a small hamlet along the left bank of the Beas River. This village forms a part of Dehra subdivision of Kangra district of Himachal Pradesh, the hill state of India.

items.note: Jawalamukhi, also known as Jwalamukhi or Jawala-ji, is a temple town and a nagar parishad in Kangra district in the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. It is the location of the Jwalamukhi temple, which is known for a naturally burning flame emanating from a rock fissure, and recognized as one of the Shakta pithas.
Naleti is a small hamlet along the left bank of the Beas River. This village forms a part of Dehra subdivision of Kangra district of Himachal Pradesh, the hill state of India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Jwala Ji Kangra?
faq.a: The best time to visit Jwala Ji Kangra is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Jwala Ji Kangra?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kangra Airport (Gaggal), Dharamshala (33 km) and the nearest railway station is Ludhiana Junction (116 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Jwala Ji Kangra?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Jwala Ji Kangra at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Jwala Ji Kangra?
faq.a: Stays in Jwala Ji Kangra start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Jwala Ji Kangra?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jawalamukhi, Naleti.
